column1: # status = pass  -> lua2wasm MUST match reference Lua 5.5
# status = xfail -> captured bug; lua2wasm currently does NOT match. When it
#                   starts matching, the harness fails (XPASS) -> promote to pass.

column3: int/float arithmetic, bitwise, wraparound
string lib: case, sub, rep, format, match, gsub-fn
table lib + pairs/sort iteration
closures, varargs, multi-return, pcall
exponent-form float no longer gets a spurious ".0"
negative zero keeps its sign
%g switches to exponent form below 1e-4
floats print enough digits to round-trip

column1: # Fixed: pattern-engine empty matches (short-circuit guard + e!=lastmatch) ---

column3: gsub with "" pattern no longer traps
empty-match " *" no longer doubles separators
match/find with "" pattern no longer traps

column1: # Fixed: stdlib gaps (concat range, hex floats, next nil) -------------------

column3: table.concat honors i/j range args
tonumber parses hex floats
next({}) returns nil
math.log base 2/10 uses log2/log10
1^inf is 1.0
(f()) is adjusted to a single value
%q emits backslash + real newline

column1: # Fixed: int //0 and %0 raise a catchable error (were nil payloads) ---------
# (semantic check only — exact error wording is not asserted, by design)

column3: int // 0 raises a catchable string error
int % 0 raises a catchable string error
arithmetic on a non-number raises a catchable string error

column1: # Fixed: spec-corner batch (found by the corner-case sweep) -----------------

column3: numeric for terminates on integer overflow (was infinite loop)
%d on a non-integer raises a catchable error (was silent 0)
%q emits type-preserving literals for number/bool/nil
int/float type, key normalization, mixed compare, tointeger
compare/length with NUL, sub/rep/byte, format combos, tonumber
classes, frontier, sets, captures, backrefs, gsub fn/table
insert/remove/move/sort/pack/unpack/ipairs
index/newindex/eq/lt/le/len/concat/call/tostring/arith
select, multi-assign, and/or/not, goto, break, repeat, pcall/xpcall

column1: # Fixed: `global` is a contextual keyword, usable as an ordinary name --------

column3: `global = 5`, `global.x`, `t.global` accepted (not forced into a decl)

column1: # Fixed: error(nil)/error() yields the string "<no error object>" (Lua 5.5) --

column3: nil error object becomes "<no error object>" at pcall/xpcall boundary

column1: # Fixed: indexing a non-table raises an "index" error, not a "call" error ----

column3: index of nil/number is a catchable error mentioning "index"

column1: # Fixed: method calls in tail position are proper tail calls (constant stack) -

column3: `return self:m(...)` is TCO'd like `return f(...)`

column1: # Fixed: table.concat rejects non-string/number elements (was silently coerced)

column3: concat of a boolean/table element is a catchable error

column1: # Fixed: multiple-assignment eval order (LHS before stores; right-to-left) -----

column3: t[i] captures old i; repeated target keeps leftmost value

column1: # Fixed: for-loop control var is fresh per iteration (closures capture distinct)

column3: closures over a for-loop variable see per-iteration values

column1: # Fixed: numeric-for is float when init or step is float (limit type irrelevant)

column3: `for i=1,3,1.0` makes i a float from the first iteration

column1: # Fixed: utf8.codes iterator — out-of-range pos -> nil (was a trap), strict errors

column3: codes iterator stops on out-of-range pos; invalid seq is catchable

column1: # Fixed: gmatch empty matches suppressed when end == previous end (e~=lastmatch)

column3: gmatch no longer doubles/ trails spurious empty matches

column1: # Fixed: tostring of table/function uses "type: 0xADDR" (was bare "table")

column3: tostring shape/stability/distinctness for tables and functions

column1: # Fixed: getmetatable(string) returns the shared string metatable (was nil)

column3: strings expose {__index = string} via getmetatable

column1: # Fixed: setmetatable type-checks args (was an illegal-cast trap on non-tables)

column3: setmetatable on a non-table is a catchable error

column1: # Fixed: tonumber validates base in [2,36] (out-of-range was silently nil)

column3: tonumber with an out-of-range base is a catchable error
utf8.offset two-value return; out-of-bounds/continuation are errors

column1: # Fixed: utf8.len/utf8.codepoint validate positions (out-of-range was clamped)

column3: len/codepoint raise "out of bounds" instead of clamping

column1: # Fixed: utf8.char accepts 0..0x7FFFFFFF with extended 4-6 byte encoding

column3: utf8.char range/byte-counts incl. >U+10FFFF (was capped at 0x10FFFF)

column1: # Fixed: string.format %p ("0x<addr>" for ref types, "(null)" otherwise)

column3: %p formats address-bearing values vs (null), was echoed literally
os.date supports name/12-hour/%c/%x specifiers (UTC, deterministic)

column1: # Fixed: os.time(table) builds a timestamp from a date table (was ignored)

column3: os.time(table) round-trips via os.date; required-field/type errors
float tostring widened to %.15g; %.17g fallback when needed

column1: # Fixed: table.insert/remove validate position bounds and arity (was silent/trap)

column3: insert/remove raise on out-of-range pos / wrong arity

column1: # Found by the 2026-05-21 sweep over README "incomplete/partial features" and
# "runtime sharp edges". Note: error-message *wording* and chunk-name
# formatting are intentionally not tracked; only semantic behavior.
# -- fixed in this sweep (now regression anchors) --

column3: math.* coerce numeric strings; non-number raises catchable "number expected"
function tostring/%p carries a stable, distinct per-object address
order-compare error names operand types (two table values / table with number)
__name renames an object in tostring and type-aware compare errors
deep non-tail recursion raises a catchable "stack overflow"
os.difftime(t2,t1) returns the second difference as a float
os.setlocale query/"C" returns "C"
io.input()/io.output() return default handles; io.write routes through them
min/max use the full < operator; ult coerces int strings, rejects non-integers

column1: # -- deferred: large milestone (open a discussion before tackling) --
# Full <close> on early exits needs close calls injected before every
# return/break/goto crossing a close scope PLUS try_table error-path unwinding
# — invasive changes to control-flow and exception lowering. Niche feature;
# natural-block-exit close already works. Kept captured.

column3: <close> runs __close on return/break/goto/error exits, reverse order

column1: # -- blocked by hard constraints / missing metadata (kept captured) --

column3: tostring(0/0) sign is platform-dependent (WASM canonicalises NaN positive)
__gc finalizers impossible: no bundled GC, no WasmGC finalization hook
__mode weak tables impossible: WasmGC has no weak references
no managed GC to report live memory for "count"
nparams/nups/isvararg/linedefined need codegen debug metadata we don't retain
Lua 5.5 strict-global rejection is compile-time (harness sees <compile-fail>)
reject goto into a local's scope — compile-time parity (needs parser machinery)
fuzzer-found: float fmod precision for large magnitudes
fuzzer-found: float % zero-sign follows dividend, not always +0
